headline: Notre Dame Flexes, Texas Survives, Arkansas Arrives
subhead: Week 8 delivered statement wins from the elite tier while chaos rumbled through the middle of the field.
lead: Notre Dame's 58-14 obliteration of Penn State announced itself as a top-tier program with no apologies, but the real story of Week 8 was Texas slipping past Miami in a one-point thriller that tested whether the power poll's top team has the resilience to hold that perch. Meanwhile, Arkansas's 55-31 demolition of Clemson suggests the SEC is producing not one but two genuinely dangerous programs.

segments.title: Notre Dame Delivers the Week's Signature Dominance
segments.body: Fifty-eight points. That scoreline doesn't arrive by accident. Notre Dame suffocated Penn State from the opening possession and never relented, turning what looked like a marquee matchup into a statement of intent. This is a team operating on a different level right now, and the 44-point margin left no room for debate about where the Irish stand in the pecking order.

segments.title: Boston College Sneaks Past Oregon in Pac Shocker
segments.body: Boston College walked out with a 28-25 win against a team that was supposed to be climbing the ladder. Oregon had the looks of a program finding rhythm, but Boston College's defense tightened when it mattered most and pulled off a three-point road upset that scrambles the West Coast picture considerably.

segments.title: Arkansas Gatecrashes the Elite Conversation
segments.body: Arkansas's 55-31 throttling of Clemson wasn't close enough to be a shootout and wasn't defensive enough to be a slugfest—it was simply superior execution at every level. With that victory, Arkansas has climbed into the top four and now sits directly behind Texas and Tennessee in the pecking order. The question isn't whether Arkansas belongs in elite company anymore; it's whether the committee can ignore them.

segments.title: LSU Edges UCLA in West Coast Artillery Duel
segments.body: Forty-one to thirty-seven. LSU held off UCLA in a game where both offenses kept firing, and LSU simply landed the final punch. The Tigers remain entrenched in the top five and proved they can win shootouts against talented opponents who refuse to quit.

segments.title: Texas Survives the Scare, Questions Linger
segments.body: Texas scraped past Miami 23-22, a one-point escape that shouldn't inspire confidence in a team sitting atop the power poll. Miami fought tooth and nail, and while Texas's ability to survive close games is a strength, this result will get plenty of scrutiny in rooms where precision matters most. Ranked teams are supposed to put away games like this, not survive them.

closer: Next week's schedule will test whether the tier one programs can maintain separation from a middle field that's proven it can surprise anyone paying attention.
